---
title: "Estrup_aviser"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-11"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)
library(ggwordcloud)

#load sentida 
if(!require("devtools")) install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("Guscode/Sentida")
library(Sentida)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}

#load our data
Estrup_efter_attentat <- read_csv("data/Estrup_efter_attentat.csv")
Estrup_før_attentat <- read_csv("data/Estrup_før_attentat.csv")

```



```{r}

#how many papers wrote about estrup in the period?
unique(Estrup_før_attentat$familyId)

length(unique(Estrup_før_attentat$familyId))
length(unique(Estrup_efter_attentat$familyId))

#8 papers in total 
```


```{r}
#load stopword list
dk_stopwords <- read.csv("stopord - max/stopord_18.csv", header = FALSE)

colnames(dk_stopwords) <- "word"

```


```{r}
word_freq_før <- Estrup_før_attentat %>%
  select(fulltext_org) %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, fulltext_org) %>%  
  anti_join(dk_stopwords, by = "word") %>% 
  filter(str_detect(word, "[a-zæøå]")) %>% 
  count(word, sort = TRUE)

head(word_freq_før, 20)


word_freq_efter <- Estrup_efter_attentat %>%
  select(fulltext_org) %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, fulltext_org) %>%   
  anti_join(dk_stopwords, by = "word") %>% 
  filter(str_detect(word, "[a-zæøå]")) %>% 
  count(word, sort = TRUE)

head(word_freq_efter, 20)

```


```{r}

# Word Clouds 
ggplot(word_freq_før %>% head(30), aes(label = word, size = n)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud() +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 15) +
  labs(title = "Most used words before assasination attempt") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(word_freq_efter %>% head(30), aes(label = word, size = n)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud() +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 15) +
  labs(title = "Most used words after assasination attempt") +
  theme_minimal()

# decided not to use this in final project, but will leave it here to show process
```
```{r}

# Time for sentiment analysis with Sentida 
sentiment_før <- Estrup_før_attentat %>%
  mutate(sentiment = map_dbl(fulltext_org, ~sentida(.x, output = "mean")),
         period = "før")

sentiment_efter <- Estrup_efter_attentat %>%
  mutate(sentiment = map_dbl(fulltext_org, ~sentida(.x, output = "mean")),
         period = "efter")


bind_rows(sentiment_før, sentiment_efter) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = period, y = sentiment, fill = period)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Sentiment i avisdækning af Estrup",
       x = "Periode", y = "Gennemsnitlig sentiment") +
  theme_minimal()


# -------------------
#Sentiment per newspaper per period
sentiment_før <- Estrup_før_attentat %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment = map_dbl(fulltext_org, ~sentida(.x, output = "mean")),
    period = "før"
  )

sentiment_efter <- Estrup_efter_attentat %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment = map_dbl(fulltext_org, ~sentida(.x, output = "mean")),
    period = "efter"
  )

sentiment_combined <- bind_rows(sentiment_før, sentiment_efter)


#Average sentiment per newspaper per period 
sentiment_by_paper <- sentiment_combined %>%
  group_by(familyId, period) %>%
  summarise(mean_sentiment = mean(sentiment, na.rm = TRUE),
            n_articles = n(),
            .groups = "drop")

```


```{r}
# Average sentiment per newspaper per period
sentiment_by_paper <- sentiment_combined %>%
  group_by(familyId, period) %>%
  summarise(mean_sentiment = mean(sentiment, na.rm = TRUE),
            n_articles = n(),
            .groups = "drop")

# Time to calculate the CHANGE in sentiment
sentiment_change <- sentiment_by_paper %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, 
              values_from = c(mean_sentiment, n_articles)) %>%
  mutate(sentiment_change = mean_sentiment_efter - `mean_sentiment_før`) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(sentiment_change)))

print(sentiment_change)
```


```{r}


# Time to visualise which papers changed most? 
sentiment_change %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(familyId, sentiment_change), 
             y = sentiment_change,
             fill = sentiment_change > 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "darkgreen", "FALSE" = "red"),
                    labels = c("TRUE" = "More positive", "FALSE" = "More negative")) +
  labs(
    title = "change in each papers sentiment after the assasination attempt",
    subtitle = "Positive = more positive tone afterwards",
    x = NULL,
    y = "change in average sentiment",
    fill = "Direction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# Sentiment before vs after per paper
sentiment_by_paper %>%
  mutate(period = factor(period, levels = c("før", "efter"))) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = period, y = mean_sentiment, 
             group = familyId, color = familyId)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  labs(
    title = "Sentiment pr. paper: before vs. after assasination attempt",
    x = "Period",
    y = "Average sentiment",
    color = "Paper"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}

# they are almost all the same sentiment - lets see if it changes by  analysing only sentences mentioning Estrup


#  Extract only sentences mentioning Estrup 
extract_estrup_sentences <- function(text) {
  sentences <- unlist(str_split(text, "(?<=[.!?])\\s+"))
  estrup_sentences <- sentences[str_detect(sentences, 
                                        regex("estrup", ignore_case = TRUE))]
  paste(estrup_sentences, collapse = " ")
}

# Apply to both datasets
sentiment_før <- Estrup_før_attentat %>%
  mutate(
    estrup_text = map_chr(fulltext_org, extract_estrup_sentences),
    sentiment   = map_dbl(estrup_text, ~{
      if (nchar(.x) < 5) NA_real_     
      else sentida(.x, output = "mean")
    }),
    period = "før"
  )

sentiment_efter <- Estrup_efter_attentat %>%
  mutate(
    estrup_text = map_chr(fulltext_org, extract_estrup_sentences),
    sentiment   = map_dbl(estrup_text, ~{
      if (nchar(.x) < 5) NA_real_
      else sentida(.x, output = "mean")
    }),
    period = "efter"
  )


# now its time to try again
# Combine the two periods (now using Estrup-filtered sentiment)
sentiment_combined <- bind_rows(sentiment_før, sentiment_efter)

# Average sentiment per newspaper per period 
sentiment_by_paper <- sentiment_combined %>%
  filter(!is.na(sentiment)) %>%   
  group_by(familyId, period) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_sentiment = mean(sentiment, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_articles     = n(),
    .groups        = "drop"
  )

# Calculate the change
sentiment_change <- sentiment_by_paper %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from  = period,
              values_from = c(mean_sentiment, n_articles)) %>%
  mutate(sentiment_change = mean_sentiment_efter - `mean_sentiment_før`) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(sentiment_change)))

print(sentiment_change)

# Chart 1
sentiment_change %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(familyId, sentiment_change),
             y = sentiment_change,
             fill = sentiment_change > 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "darkgreen", "FALSE" = "red"),
                    labels = c("TRUE" = "More positive", "FALSE" = "More negative")) +
  labs(
    title = "Change in sentiment after assasination attempt pr. paper",
    subtitle = "Only sentences mentioning Estrup",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Ændring i sentiment (efter − før)",
    fill = "Retning"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Chart 2
sentiment_by_paper %>%
  mutate(period = factor(period, levels = c("før", "efter"))) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = period, y = mean_sentiment, 
             group = familyId, color = familyId)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  labs(
    title = "Sentiment pr. paper: before vs. after assasination attempt",
    subtitle = "Only sentences mentioning Estrup",
    x = "Period",
    y = "Average sentiment",
    color = "Paper"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```




```{r}

# Combine both periods with dates
sentiment_combined <- bind_rows(sentiment_før, sentiment_efter) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sentiment))

# Make sure timestamp is in date format
sentiment_combined <- sentiment_combined %>%
  mutate(timestamp = as.Date(timestamp))

# Plot
ggplot(sentiment_combined, aes(x = timestamp, y = sentiment, color = familyId)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("1885-10-21"),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("1885-10-21"), y = max(sentiment_combined$sentiment, na.rm = TRUE),
           label = "assassination attempt", hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5, color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Sentiment in newspapers coverage of Estrup over time",
    subtitle = "Only sentences mentioning Estrup",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Sentiment",
    color = "Paper"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()




# Plot with only two (very different) newspapers
sentiment_combined %>%
  filter(familyId %in% c("aktuelt", "berlingsketidende")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = timestamp, y = sentiment, color = familyId)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("1885-10-21"),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("1885-10-21"), y = max(sentiment_combined$sentiment, na.rm = TRUE),
           label = "assassination attempt", hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5, color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Sentiment in newspapers coverage of Estrup over time",
    subtitle = "Only sentences mentioning Estrup",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Sentiment",
    color = "Paper"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
# the graph above shows that there is not a big difference over time and much of it is neutral
# lets explore why by using some examples


# examples on words 
sentida("socialisme", output = "mean")

sentida("konge", output = "mean")

sentida("demokrati", output = "mean")


# example on sentence
sentida("Ogsaa Estrup har jo Friheden paa sit Program", output = "total")


# Sentida can't read OCR proberly
sentida("pr. estrup var dum", output = "mean")

sentida("dernæst talte redaktør laursen om at minister estrup var dum", output = "mean")


# example on neutral sentence
sentida("Til Fanetoget\nfor Conseilsprasident Estrup ndleie flag", output = "mean")

sentida("Til Fanetoget for Estrup udlejes flag", output = "mean")
```


```{r}


```
